beats.speaker: BOB BAITER
beats.text:
Ladies and Gentlemen!
I'm Lake Ulysses
councilman Bob Baiter!

beats.speaker: BOB BAITER
beats.text:
It's my pleasure to announce
after nearly a decade,

beats.speaker: BOB BAITER
beats.text:
boating and fishing
are once again permitted
throughout our beautiful
three-thousand acre jewel!

beats.speaker: BOB BAITER
beats.text:
City council has been carefully
monitoring toxicity levels.

beats.speaker: BOB BAITER
beats.text:
With recent rains
and cooler temperatures
we are pleased to report that

beats.speaker: BOB BAITER
BOB BAITER
beats.text:
You know what that means!
Your family and friends are

beats.speaker: BOB BAITER
beats.text:
legally allowed to return
to our blue pride and joy!

beats.speaker: BOB BAITER
beats.text:
Families are advised
not to drink the water,

beats.speaker: BOB BAITER
beats.text:
or allow your pets
to enter the lake.

beats.speaker: BOB BAITER
beats.text:
Pregnant women are cautioned
to keep their skin covered
at all times.

beats.speaker: BOB BAITER
beats.text:
give your catch of the day
a good scrubbin' with
some fresh bottled water!

beats.speaker: BOB BAITER
beats.text:
From live bait
to the latest fishing gear!

beats.speaker: BOB BAITER
beats.text:
Whether you're local
or just passin' by —

beats.speaker: BOB BAITER
beats.text:
Now let's go enjoy
our watery playground!
